channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年07月04日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
item.description: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, enc

item.title: [未译] 'Start work at 11' - but will other bosses be as flexible over England's 1am match?
item.description: Employers are being urged to use their "common sense" to allow staff to work flexibly where they can.

item.title: [未译] Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
item.description: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.

item.title: [未译] Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
item.description: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

item.title: [未译] Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
item.description: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

item.title: [未译] No-gift policy for Taylor Swift, but how much should you give at a wedding?
item.description: Wedding lists are being replaced by cash requests, but guests are divided over how much to give.

item.title: [未译] The portrait shedding light on Spain’s decisive role in US independence: ‘This is proof that we are founders, not outsiders’
item.description: Growing recognition of the military leader Bernardo de Gálvez is helping highlight the role played by Spain and Latin America in the founding of the United States

item.title: [未译] Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
item.description: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.

item.title: [未译] The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work.

item.title: [未译] The United States on María Corina Machado’s attempts to return to Venezuela: They are ‘counterproductive’
item.description: Edmundo González, the opposition candidate in the 2024 election, insists the Nobel Peace Prize laureate has the right to return

item.title: [未译] Venture Capital During the AI Revolution: Masters in Business with Mamoon Hamid
item.description: Barry sits down with Kleiner Perkins Partner, Mamoon Hamid. They discuss Mamoon's thoughts on the AI revolution and his approach to early AI investing. Mamoon also breaks down how he became an early

item.title: [未译] 1ドル200円の円安シナリオ、高市氏インド訪問、W杯ピッチ外の戦い─1週間のニュース5選
item.description: マーケットで話題になったニュースから、1週間を振り返る五つのトピックを厳選して紹介します。

item.title: [未译] Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
item.description: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

item.title: [未译] CPP Invests $1.75 Billion in EQT’s AI Buildout
item.description: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board will invest $1.75 billion to support EQT AB’s artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout, it announced Friday.

item.title: [未译] Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
item.description: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.

item.title: [未译] Toronto Stocks Enjoy Broad Gains After New Pipeline Proposal
item.description: Canada’s stocks benchmark climbed Friday morning after Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled a new pipeline project to the country’s West Coast.

item.title: [未译] Extreme heat wave threatens U.S. power grids and July 4 travel
item.description: The heat wave threatens to overwhelm U.S. grids and may force some to change their plans on one of the busiest travel weeks of the year.

item.title: [未译] U.S. Officials Believed Israel Was Plotting to Kill Iranian Negotiators
item.description: Any Israeli attempt to kill Abbas Araghchi, the foreign minister, or Mohammad Ghalibaf, the Parliament speaker, would have derailed peace talks, American officials feared.

item.title: [未译] Burnham says there is some room for movement on tax
item.description: But the likely next PM says he will stick to Labour's pledges to not raise VAT, income tax or national insurance.

item.title: [未译] 'Less than 10% sheep': How millions may have unknowingly eaten goat, skin and fat kebabs
item.description: Millions are likely to have eaten "lamb" kebabs that were actually made with goat, skin and fat.

item.title: [未译] France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
item.description: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

item.title: [未译] Tackle workplace sickness to unlock hidden growth, former John Lewis boss says
item.description: More than 250 of the UK's biggest employers have signed up to his 'Get Britain Working' taskforce.

item.title: [未译] Rocket Report: Indian startup nears first launch; SpaceX's millenary milestone
item.description: NASA awarded Rocket Lab deals for three dedicated launches using the company's Electron rocket.

item.title: [未译] Why Some Banks Still Charge High Overdraft Fees
item.description: Congress scrapped a rule last year that capped how much banks could charge if you spent more than was in your account. Now, banks’ revenue from the fees is rising.

item.title: [未译] Copper Tracks Dollar Swings as Traders Weigh US Rate Outlook
item.description: Copper rose on Friday, tracking small swings in the US dollar as investors continue to assess the outlook for US monetary policy.

item.title: [未译] A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
item.description: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the n

item.title: [未译] Gold Heads for First Weekly Gain Since May on Easing Fed Outlook
item.description: Gold headed for its first weekly gain since May as traders dialed down their expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes.

item.title: [未译] AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence?
item.description: Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system.

item.title: [未译] More than 400 dead in DR Congo's Ebola outbreak
item.description: In tonight's edition, Ebola is spreading beyond its original hotspot in the DRC as a new case has been detected nearly 600 kilometers away. And another human rights catastrophe is unfolding in Sudan a

item.title: [未译] German row over plan for workers to need sick note on first day of illness
item.description: A doctors' group says it "borders on madness" that patients will have to obtain the note in person.
